summary: Tested the white-label branding feature end-to-end on a fresh DB. Backend: 16/16 branding/auth/share tests pass (setup wizard, public GET /api/settings, PUT /api/admin/settings with auth + invalid hex rejection, logo POST/GET/DELETE, share creation, login). Frontend UI: setup wizard at /admin works with the required testids and lands on /admin/dashboard; dashboard shows business name 'Aurora Studios' + platform-footer; Settings → Branding tab is default, pre-fills, saving updates --brand CSS var and persists across reload; logo upload + remove work; login page + share page (/s/<token>) both show the business name and the fixed platform credit footer; CSS --brand propagates to share page. Existing admin login (admin/test123) still works after setup. However, real white-label leakage exists via the fallback assets — see critical findings.

ui_bugs.issue: When no custom logo is uploaded, every page (login, dashboard, settings, share view, guest upload) renders the static file /app/frontend/public/logo.png as the header logo. That file is the original 'Weddings By Mark' handwritten-signature image, so any client visiting a fresh white-label tenant's share gallery sees the original brand. The branding code paths are correct (logoSrc falls back to '/logo.png'), but the fallback asset itself is brand-specific and must be replaced with a generic neutral placeholder (or a wordmark generated from business_name).

ShareView.js line 44: watermarkSrc = branding.has_custom_logo ? logoSrc : '/watermark-logo.png'. /app/frontend/public/watermark-logo.png is again the 'Weddings By Mark' signature, so client photos are watermarked with the original brand whenever the photographer hasn't uploaded a logo. Replace with neutral default or skip watermark when has_custom_logo is false.

Hardcoded email mark@perfectweddingsbymark.uk on line 1044 (the 'I'm sorry…' help block) has NO fallback to branding.contact_email — line 476 uses the fallback correctly, but 1044 is a literal string. White-label tenants will leak the original photographer's email.

Hardcoded instructional text 'Your Wedding Album (If Included in Your Package)' with Mark-specific wording (album favourites flow, gold heart, '40 favourites') is rendered to every white-label tenant's clients. Make this either configurable per-tenant or generic.

action_items: Replace /app/frontend/public/logo.png with a neutral white-label placeholder (or render a text wordmark from branding.business_name when has_custom_logo is false) so the original 'Weddings By Mark' signature does not appear on any unconfigured tenant's pages.
Replace /app/frontend/public/watermark-logo.png with a neutral default, or skip watermarking entirely when has_custom_logo is false.
ShareView.js:1044 — use branding.contact_email (with a generic fallback like 'your photographer') instead of the hardcoded mark@perfectweddingsbymark.uk.
Make ShareView.js 'Your Wedding Album' instructional block configurable per-tenant (or remove the Mark-specific wording) so it does not appear verbatim on every tenant's client galleries.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 3105 lines — strongly consider splitting (routers per resource, services for FFmpeg/branding) for maintainability.
Branding fallback logic in branding.js is clean and the BrandingProvider correctly sets --brand and --brand-rgb CSS vars on document root; document.title also updates from business_name. Good.
build_branding_response() correctly excludes _id and returns a stable public shape including platform_credit; PUT validates hex with regex and rejects bad input with 400.
Setup endpoint is idempotent (second call → 400). Login still works post-setup. Auth guard on PUT/POST/DELETE branding endpoints is enforced.
Hardcoded brand assets and strings (logo.png, watermark-logo.png, ShareView email/instructions) are the only place where the white-label promise is broken — backend/contract is solid.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested flows (wizard → dashboard → settings → logo → login → share). Brand-leakage findings above are content-asset issues, not flow failures.

test_credentials: admin / test123 (created via wizard with business_name=Aurora Studios, accent=#7C3AED). DB reset to empty during testing then re-seeded by the wizard.
seed_data_creation: Wizard-created admin (Aurora Studios). A TEST_UI_Share2 gallery + no-password share were created during UI testing and have been deleted afterwards. No leftover TEST_ data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: DB has been left with admin user 'admin'/'test123' and business_name='Aurora Studios', accent_color='#22C55E' (the UI test saved that value as part of its happy path). No galleries remain. Re-run backend tests with: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_branding.py -v. To re-test the wizard UI itself you'll need to wipe db.admins/db.settings/db.templates first.
rca of the issue: White-label leakage RCA: the new BrandingProvider correctly resolves logoSrc to '/logo.png' (and ShareView's watermark to '/watermark-logo.png') when has_custom_logo is false. Both files in /app/frontend/public/ are still the original Weddings By Mark signature PNGs. ShareView also embeds the original photographer's email and Mark-specific 'wedding album favourites' instructions directly in the JSX. Mitigation: swap the two PNGs for neutral defaults (or render a CSS/SVG wordmark from branding.business_name when no custom logo), replace the hardcoded mailto on line 1044 with branding.contact_email, and parameterise (or remove) the Mark-specific instructional copy in ShareView.
